daily_brief: The US-Israel war against Iran entered its third week with no diplomatic off-ramp in sight, as President Trump rejected mediation efforts and threatened further strikes on Iran's Kharg Island oil hub [Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi warned countries against joining the conflict, responding to Trump's call for allies to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz [Le Monde, France 24]. The war's regional toll mounted: Gulf states intercepted Iranian missiles and drones [Globe and Mail], while Israeli strikes on Lebanon killed over 800 people as Hezbollah claimed ground clashes with Israeli forces near Khiam [BBC, Le Monde]. The Pentagon identified six US service members killed in a KC-135 crash in Iraq [BBC, Le Monde]. In Europe, Ukrainian President Zelensky accused EU allies of 'blackmail' over demands to repair the Druzhba oil pipeline carrying Russian crude to Hungary [BBC]. France held the first round of municipal elections seen as a barometer ahead of 2027 presidential polls [RFI, BBC], while the death of German philosopher Jürgen Habermas at 96 marked the passing of a towering intellectual figure [BBC]. Severe flooding in Kenya killed at least 62 people as climate extremes continued to grip East Africa [BBC, RFI].

top_stories.headline: Trump Rejects Iran Ceasefire Talks as War Enters Third Week, Calls on Allies to Escort Gulf Tankers
bullets: President Trump rejected mediation efforts from Middle Eastern allies and said the US is not ready to negotiate, stating terms are 'not good enough yet' and hinting at further strikes on Iran's Kharg Island oil hub

Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi said the war will only end when Tehran is certain attacks cannot be repeated and reparations are paid; Iran warned countries against actions that could escalate the conflict
Trump called on China, France, Japan, South Korea, and the UK to send warships to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz; responses from named countries were non-committal
The war has killed more than 2,000 people, mostly in Iran, and created the largest-ever oil supply disruption as maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has halted

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized diplomatic rejection from both Washington and Tehran, detailed mediation attempts by Oman

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Led with Trump's statement that he's 'not ready' for a deal; included Trump's dismissive comments about Iran's new Supreme Leader

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Focused on Iran's warnings to other countries and the guarded reception to Trump's escort request

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Video coverage emphasizing Trump's call for allied warships

top_stories.headline: Israeli Strikes Kill Over 800 in Lebanon as Hezbollah Reports Ground Clashes with IDF
bullets: Lebanese health ministry reported 826 deaths including 106 children since Israeli strikes resumed on March 2, with Hezbollah claiming 'direct clashes' with Israeli forces in the town of Khiam
US outlet Axios reported Israel is planning a major ground invasion to seize the area south of the Litani River; the region is already under Israeli evacuation orders
UN Secretary-General Guterres visited Beirut and said diplomatic channels remain open, urging international support for Lebanon
France's President Macron offered to mediate direct talks between Lebanon and Israel, saying Lebanese leaders are willing to engage

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Detailed reporting from strike aftermath in Younine; noted Israeli military's claim of targeting 'Hezbollah terrorist infrastructure'

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Hezbollah's statement about ongoing ground clashes; reported on potential Israeli invasion plans

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on French mediation offer and characterized it as a 'major shift' in Lebanon's approach to Israel

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Reported on civilian toll at mass funeral in Sidon; explained 'Dahiya Doctrine' strategy

top_stories.headline: Zelensky Accuses EU Allies of 'Blackmail' Over Russian Oil Pipeline Dispute
bullets: Ukrainian President Zelensky said EU pressure to repair the Soviet-era Druzhba pipeline, damaged by Russian strikes in January, amounts to blackmail and is equivalent to lifting sanctions on Moscow
Hungary is blocking €90 billion in EU loans to Ukraine and fresh sanctions on Russia until the pipeline is reopened; PM Orban has made hostility to Ukraine a campaign issue ahead of April elections
The EU has asked Ukraine to allow inspectors to assess the pipeline damage; some EU officials fear Zelensky's resistance could help Orban win re-election
Zelensky said Ukraine does not want to lose US support while Washington is 'preoccupied with the Middle East' and offered to share drone expertise with US allies in the region

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Neutral presentation of dispute; noted Hungary's dependence on Russian energy

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Ukraine's concern about losing US backing due to the Iran war

top_stories.headline: France Holds First Round of Municipal Elections Ahead of 2027 Presidential Race
bullets: Polling stations opened across France for the first of two rounds of municipal elections to choose councillors for 34,875 municipalities
Midday turnout in metropolitan France was 19.37 percent, one point higher than the first round in 2020 which was disrupted by COVID-19
The Paris mayorship is the highest-profile contest, with the city under left-wing control for 25 years but potentially shifting to the right
Results will be analyzed for trends ahead of 2027 presidential elections; President Macron cannot run again after serving two consecutive terms

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on turnout and context for presidential implications

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Emphasized polarization in French politics and role of alliances between mainstream and far-right/far-left parties

top_stories.headline: Severe Flooding Kills at Least 62 in Kenya as Climate Extremes Grip East Africa
bullets: At least 62 people have died in Kenya over the past week from flooding caused by heavy rains, up from 42 reported a week earlier
More than half the deaths occurred in Nairobi, where poor drainage and unregulated development blocking waterways worsened conditions; over 2,000 people displaced
Kenya Red Cross rescued 11 people from a flooded minibus and two children from a flooded house overnight; roads closed and schools flooded in the capital
Neighboring Somalia faces severe drought, with communities experiencing years of failed rains and shrinking wells while Kenya suffers flooding

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Focused on rescue operations and infrastructure failures in Nairobi

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Broader regional framing contrasting Kenya's floods with Somalia's drought as manifestations of climate change

additional_stories.headline: German philosopher Jürgen Habermas, leading figure of the Frankfurt School and critic of capitalism, dies at 96
Pentagon identifies six US crew members killed in KC-135 refueling aircraft crash in western Iraq
Israeli forces kill Palestinian couple and two children in occupied West Bank; military says incident under investigation
ICC judge says US sanctions over Netanyahu arrest warrant left her without bank cards and Google accounts
Congo-Brazzaville holds elections expected to extend 82-year-old President Sassou Nguesso's four-decade rule
France returns sacred Djidji Ayôkwé talking drum looted from Côte d'Ivoire over 100 years ago
UK government axes Global Health Workforce Programme supporting healthcare training in six African countries due to aid cuts
Turkey warns US and Israel against involving Iran's Kurdish minority in Middle East war
India's External Affairs Minister Jaishankar begins two-day Brussels visit for talks with EU foreign ministers
Pakistan says it struck military facilities and 'terrorist hideouts' in southern Afghanistan including near Kandahar
